content.full_text:
Re:
F ern P olrrrcel P RACTTcEs C ourrarssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February L9,2OO9
Linda Janson
Executive Assistant to
Councilmember Sherri S. Lightner
202 C Street
San Diego, CAgzlOl
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. A-09-ffi3
Dear Ms. Janson:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of San Diego city
Councilmember Sherri S. Lightner regarding her duties concerning the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is based on the
facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act
as the finder of fact when it renders advice. (ln re oglesby (1975) I FppC ops. 71.)
Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws
such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
Do the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions preclude Councilmember Lightner
from considering and voting on the proposed development project known as the Hillel
Project in La Jolla due to her prior positions as President of Taxpayers for Responsible
Land Use, Inc., President of La Jolla Town Council, Inc., President of La Jolla Shores
Association, and a director of the La Jolla Community Planning Association, [nc.?
' The Political Retbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8ll0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Califbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
=-


File No. 4-09-003
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
Councilmember Lightner does not have an economic interest in any of these
organizations for purposes of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions. Therefore, her
service on or association with these organizations do not preclude her from voting on or
participating in decisions on the Hillel project.
FACTS
You are writing on behalf of Councilmember Sherri Lightner. Before
Ms. Lightner was elected to the San Diego city council, she served with many
community organizations including serving as President of the La Jolla Town Council,
Inc, President of the La Jolla Shores Association, a director of the La Jolla Community
Planning Association, fnc., and President of Taxpayers for Responsible Land Use, [nc.,
("TFRLU"). While serving as President of TFRLU, the Hillel Project was passed by the
city council and then blocked by a Superior court lawsuit filed bylhe TFRi_u.
Councilmember Lightner quit her position with the TFRLU in 2006 and has not retumed.
According to the facts that you gave us during our phone conversation on February 10,
2009, Councilmember Lightner never received payment from any of the co*munity
organizations which she served and has no property within 500 feet of the proposed
project.
AIIALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will "perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making or
otherwise using his or her official position to influence governmental decisions in which
the official has a financial interest, unless an exception applies.
The Commission has adopted an eighrsbp standard analysis to decide whether an
official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. (Regulation lg70b@) (t)-(g).) The
general rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes
a goverlmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests. (Section 87103.)
Step One: Is Councilmember Lightner a public official?
As an elected member of the San Diego City Council, Councilmember Lightner is
a public official under the Act. (Section gZ04B.)


File No. 4-09-003
Page No.4
You do describe several nonprofits that may have been a source of income to the
councilmember prior to 2006. However, these entilies would not be considered ,.sources
of income" under the Act since she received no income from these organizations.
Finally, since there are no facts given in regard to Councilmember Lightner
having a family member with an economic interesi in the Hillel project, there also seems
to not be any personal financial effect.
Absent an economic interest, the councilmember will not have a conflict of
interest in the decision. Thus, we do not analyze the remaining steps.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General
By:
oel Lawson
JLjgl
Legal Intern, Legal Division
